1в†’# Source Resolution Protocols
     2в†’
     3в†’## Remote Source Resolution (Forge/Deep only)
     4в†’
     5в†’If `source_repo` is a local path: proceed with the tier-appropriate strategy as normal.
     6в†’
     7в†’If `source_repo` is a remote URL (GitHub URL or owner/repo format) AND tier is Forge or Deep:
     8в†’
     9в†’1. **Check `git` availability:** Verify `git` is functional (`git --version`). If `git` is not available, skip to the fallback warning below.
    10в†’
    11в†’2. **Ephemeral shallow clone:** Clone the repository to a system temp path for AST access:
    12в†’
    13в†’   ```
    14в†’   temp_path = {system_temp}/skf-ephemeral-{skill-name}-{timestamp}/
    15в†’   git clone --depth 1 --branch {branch} --single-branch --filter=blob:none {source_repo} {temp_path}
    16в†’   ```
    17в†’
    18в†’   **If `include_patterns` are NOT specified:**
    19в†’
    20в†’   ```
    21в†’   git clone --depth 1 --branch {branch} --single-branch --filter=blob:none {source_repo} {temp_path}
    22в†’   ```
    23в†’
    24в†’   **If `include_patterns` ARE specified**, use sparse-checkout to limit the clone scope:
    25в†’
    26в†’   ```
    27в†’   git clone --depth 1 --branch {branch} --single-branch --filter=blob:none --sparse {source_repo} {temp_path}
    28в†’   ```
    29в†’
    30в†’   **Mode selection:** Choose sparse-checkout mode based on whether `exclude_patterns` exist:
    31в†’
    32в†’   - **No `exclude_patterns`:** Use default **cone mode** (faster). Convert `include_patterns` to directory roots.
    33в†’   - **`exclude_patterns` present:** Use **`--no-cone` mode** which supports gitignore-style negation patterns (`!` prefix). This applies both include and exclude at the git level, avoiding unnecessary blob downloads.
    34в†’
    35в†’   **Cone mode (no exclude patterns):**
    36в†’
    37в†’   **IMPORTANT:** `git sparse-checkout set` expects **directories**, not glob patterns. Convert `include_patterns` before passing them:
    38в†’
    39в†’   **Classification rule:** A pattern is an **individual file** if it contains no glob characters (`*`, `?`, `[`) AND does not end with `/`. Everything else is a glob вЂ” strip it to its directory root (the path prefix before the first glob character or wildcard segment).
    40в†’
    41в†’   - Strip glob suffixes to directory roots (e.g., `src/core/**/*.py` в†’ `src/core`, `src/api/*.ts` в†’ `src/api`)
    42в†’   - Deduplicate the resulting directory list
    43в†’   - Individual files (e.g., `pyproject.toml`, `src/utils/helpers.py`) are kept as-is
    44в†’
    45в†’   **If only directory roots (no individual files):**
    46в†’
    47в†’   ```
    48в†’   git -C {temp_path} sparse-checkout set {converted_directory_roots}
    49в†’   ```
    50в†’
    51в†’   **If any individual files are present (or mixed):**
    52в†’
    53в†’   ```
    54в†’   git -C {temp_path} sparse-checkout set --skip-checks {converted_directory_roots} {individual_files}
    55в†’   ```
    56в†’
    57в†’   Example transformation:
    58в†’   ```
    59в†’   Brief include_patterns:        sparse-checkout args:
    60в†’   src/core/**/*.py          в†’    src/core          (directory root)
    61в†’   src/api/*.ts              в†’    src/api           (directory root)
    62в†’   examples/**/*.py          в†’    examples          (directory root)
    63в†’   pyproject.toml            в†’    pyproject.toml    (individual file, needs --skip-checks)
    64в†’   src/utils/helpers.py      в†’    src/utils/helpers.py (individual file, needs --skip-checks)
    65в†’   ```
    66в†’
    67в†’   **No-cone mode (exclude patterns present):**
    68в†’
    69в†’   When `exclude_patterns` exist, use `--no-cone` mode to pass both include and exclude patterns directly as gitignore-style rules:
    70в†’
    71в†’   1. Convert `include_patterns` to gitignore-style patterns. If a pattern contains no glob characters (`*`, `?`, `[`) and does not end with `/`, append `/**` to match directory contents recursively (e.g., `cognee` в†’ `cognee/**`; `cognee/**` в†’ kept as-is).
    72в†’   2. Convert `exclude_patterns` to negation patterns by prepending `!`. Apply the same anchoring rule (e.g., `cognee/tests` в†’ `!cognee/tests/**`; `cognee/tests/**` в†’ `!cognee/tests/**`; `**/test_*` в†’ `!**/test_*`).
    73в†’   3. **CRITICAL:** List all include patterns BEFORE negated exclude patterns вЂ” git processes patterns in order and a negation can only suppress a prior inclusion.
    74в†’   4. Pass to sparse-checkout вЂ” include patterns first, then negated exclude patterns:
    75в†’
    76в†’   ```
    77в†’   git -C {temp_path} sparse-checkout set --no-cone {include_gitignore_patterns} {negated_exclude_patterns}
    78в†’   ```
    79в†’
    80в†’   Example transformation:
    81в†’   ```
    82в†’   Brief include_patterns:        Brief exclude_patterns:
    83в†’   cognee/**                      cognee/tests/**
    84в†’                                  cognee/alembic/**
    85в†’                                  **/test_*
    86в†’
    87в†’   sparse-checkout args (--no-cone):
    88в†’   'cognee/**' '!cognee/tests/**' '!cognee/alembic/**' '!**/test_*'
    89в†’   ```
    90в†’
    91в†’   **Note:** `--no-cone` mode is slower than cone mode for very large repositories but eliminates downloading excluded blobs entirely.
    92в†’
    93в†’   **Post-checkout filtering:**
    94в†’
    95в†’   After checkout, apply the original glob `include_patterns` as file-level filters when building the extraction file list вЂ” sparse-checkout gets the right directories, glob filtering narrows to the exact files. When `--no-cone` mode was used, most exclude filtering is already done at the git level, but apply `exclude_patterns` as a final pass to catch any edge cases where gitignore pattern matching diverges from the brief's glob semantics.
    96в†’
    97в†’3. **If clone succeeds:** Update the working source path to `{temp_path}` for all subsequent AST operations in this step. Proceed with the **Forge/Deep Tier** extraction strategy below. Mark `ephemeral_clone_active = true` for cleanup.
    98в†’
    99в†’4. **If clone fails (network error, auth failure, timeout):**
   100в†’
   101в†’   вљ пёЏ **Warn the user explicitly:**
   102в†’
   103в†’   "Ephemeral clone of `{source_repo}` failed: {error}. Degrading to source reading (T1-low) for this run. For T1 (AST-verified) confidence, clone the repository locally and update `source_repo` in your brief to the local path."
   104в†’
   105в†’   Proceed with Quick tier extraction strategy below. Note the degradation reason in context for the evidence report.
   106в†’
   107в†’**Ephemeral clone cleanup:** After extraction is complete for all files in scope (whether successful or partially failed), before presenting the Gate 2 summary (Section 6), if `ephemeral_clone_active`, delete the `{temp_path}` directory. Log: "Ephemeral source clone cleaned up." This ensures cleanup runs even if some extractions failed, as long as the step itself is still executing.
   108в†’
   109в†’---
   110в†’
   111в†’## Version Reconciliation (all tiers, source mode only)
   112в†’
   113в†’**If `source_type: "docs-only"`:** skip this section вЂ” no source files exist to reconcile.
   114в†’
   115в†’After the source path is accessible (local path from step-01, or ephemeral clone from above), check whether the source contains a version identifier and reconcile it with `brief.version`. Look for the first matching version file in the resolved source path:
   116в†’
   117в†’- Python: `pyproject.toml` (`[project] version`), `setup.py` (`version=`), `__version__` in `__init__.py`
   118в†’- JavaScript/TypeScript: `package.json` (`"version"`)
   119в†’- Rust: `Cargo.toml` (`[package] version`)
   120в†’- Go: `go.mod` (module version if tagged)
   121в†’
   122в†’**If a source version is found AND it differs from `brief.version`:**
   123в†’
   124в†’вљ пёЏ Warn the user: "Brief version ({brief.version}) differs from source version ({source_version}). Using source version ({source_version})."
   125в†’
   126в†’Update the working version in context to the source version. Record the mismatch in context for the evidence report (step-08).
   127в†’
   128в†’**If no version file is found or version cannot be extracted:** keep `brief.version` as-is. No warning needed.
   129в†’
   130в†’**If source is remote and accessed via Quick tier (gh_bridge, no local files):** attempt to read the version file via `gh_bridge.read_file(owner, repo, "{version_file}")` for the primary version file of the detected language. If the read fails, keep `brief.version`.
   131в†’

1в†’---
     2в†’name: 'step-03-extract'
     3в†’description: 'Tier-dependent source code extraction вЂ” AST or source reading for exports, signatures, and types'
     4в†’nextStepFile: './step-03b-fetch-temporal.md'
     5в†’extractionPatternsData: '../data/extraction-patterns.md'
     6в†’sourceResolutionData: '../data/source-resolution-protocols.md'
     7в†’---
     8в†’
     9в†’# Step 3: Extract
    10в†’
    11в†’## STEP GOAL:
    12в†’
    13в†’To extract all public exports, function signatures, type definitions, and co-import patterns from the source code using tier-appropriate tools, building a complete extraction inventory with confidence-tiered provenance citations.
    14в†’
    15в†’## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    16в†’
    17в†’### Universal Rules:
    18в†’
    19в†’- рџ“– CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    20в†’- рџЋЇ ALWAYS follow the exact instructions in the step file
    21в†’- вљ™пёЏ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    22в†’- вњ… YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    23в†’
    24в†’### Role Reinforcement:
    25в†’
    26в†’- вњ… You are a skill compilation engine performing structural extraction
    27в†’- вњ… Zero hallucination tolerance вЂ” every extracted item must trace to source code
    28в†’- вњ… Confidence tier labeling is mandatory for every extraction result
    29в†’
    30в†’### Step-Specific Rules:
    31в†’
    32в†’- рџЋЇ Focus ONLY on extracting exports, signatures, types from source code
    33в†’- рџљ« FORBIDDEN to compile SKILL.md content вЂ” that's step-05
    34в†’- рџљ« FORBIDDEN to write any output files вЂ” extraction stays in context
    35в†’- рџљ« FORBIDDEN to include content that cannot be cited to a source location
    36в†’- вљ’пёЏ Every extracted item MUST have a provenance citation: `[AST:{file}:L{line}]` or `[SRC:{file}:L{line}]`
    37в†’
    38в†’## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    39в†’
    40в†’- рџЋЇ Follow MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    41в†’- рџ’ѕ Build extraction inventory in context вЂ” do not write files
    42в†’- рџ“– Load extraction patterns data file for tier-specific strategy
    43в†’- рџљ« If an export cannot be verified, exclude it вЂ” do not guess
    44в†’
    45в†’## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    46в†’
    47в†’- Available: brief_data, tier, source_location, file_tree from step-01; ecosystem_status from step-02
    48в†’- Focus: Source code extraction and inventory building
    49в†’- Limits: Do NOT compile, assemble, or write any output
    50в†’- Dependencies: Source code must be accessible (resolved in step-01)
    51в†’
    52в†’## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    53в†’
    54в†’**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    55в†’
    56в†’### 1. Load Extraction Patterns
    57в†’
    58в†’Load `{extractionPatternsData}` completely. Identify the strategy for the current forge tier.
    59в†’
    60в†’### 2. Apply Scope Filters
    61в†’
    62в†’From the brief, apply scope and pattern filters:
    63в†’
    64в†’- `scope` вЂ” determines what to extract (e.g., "all public exports", specific modules)
    65в†’- `include_patterns` вЂ” file globs to include (if specified)
    66в†’- `exclude_patterns` вЂ” file globs to exclude (if specified)
    67в†’
    68в†’Build the filtered file list from the source tree resolved in step-01. Record the result: "**Filtered file count: {N} files in scope**" вЂ” this count is the input to the AST Extraction Protocol decision tree in the extraction patterns data file.
    69в†’
    70в†’### 3. Check for Docs-Only Mode
    71в†’
    72в†’**If `source_type: "docs-only"` in the brief data:**
    73в†’
    74в†’"**Docs-only mode:** No source code to extract. Documentation content will be fetched from `doc_urls` in step-03c."
    75в†’
    76в†’Build an empty extraction inventory with zero exports. Set `extraction_mode: "docs-only"` in context. Auto-proceed through Gate 2 (section 5) вЂ” display the empty inventory and note that T3 content will be produced by the doc-fetcher step.
    77в†’
    78в†’**If `source_type: "source"` (default):** Continue with extraction below.
    79в†’
    80в†’### 4. Execute Tier-Dependent Extraction
    81в†’
    82в†’Load `{sourceResolutionData}` completely. Follow the **Remote Source Resolution** protocol for Forge/Deep tiers (ephemeral clone, sparse-checkout, cleanup) and the **Version Reconciliation** protocol for all tiers. Apply the protocols before proceeding with the tier-specific extraction strategy below.
    83в†’
    84в†’**Quick Tier (No AST tools):**
    85в†’
    86в†’1. Use `gh_bridge.list_tree(owner, repo, branch)` to map source structure (if remote)
    87в†’2. Identify entry points: index files, main exports, public modules
    88в†’3. Use `gh_bridge.read_file(owner, repo, path)` to read each entry point
    89в†’4. Extract from source text: exported function names, parameter lists, return types
    90в†’5. Infer types from JSDoc, docstrings, type annotations
    91в†’6. Confidence: All results T1-low вЂ” `[SRC:{file}:L{line}]`
    92в†’
    93в†’**Forge/Deep Tier (AST available):**
    94в†’
    95в†’вљ пёЏ **CRITICAL:** Before executing AST extraction, load the **AST Extraction Protocol** section from `{extractionPatternsData}`. Follow the decision tree based on the file count from step-01's file tree. This determines whether to use the MCP tool, scoped YAML rules, or CLI streaming. Never use `ast-grep --json` (without `=stream`) вЂ” it loads the entire result set into memory and will fail on large codebases.
    96в†’
    97в†’1. Detect language from brief or file extensions
    98в†’2. Follow the AST Extraction Protocol decision tree from `{extractionPatternsData}`:
    99в†’   - в‰¤100 files: use `find_code()` MCP tool with `max_results` and `output_format="text"`
   100в†’   - в‰¤500 files: use `find_code_by_rule()` MCP tool with scoped YAML rules
   101в†’   - >500 files: use CLI `--json=stream` with line-by-line streaming Python вЂ” **CRITICAL:** inject the brief's `scope.exclude` patterns into the Python filter's `EXCLUDES` list (use `[]` if absent) so excluded files are discarded before consuming `head -N` slots (see template in extraction patterns data)
   102в†’3. For each export: extract function name, full signature, parameter types, return type, line number
   103в†’4. Use `ast_bridge.detect_co_imports(path, libraries[])` to find integration points
   104в†’5. Build extraction rules YAML data for reproducibility
   105в†’6. Confidence: All results T1 вЂ” `[AST:{file}:L{line}]`
   106в†’
   107в†’**If AST tool is unavailable at Forge/Deep tier:**
   108в†’
   109в†’вљ пёЏ **Warn the user explicitly:** "AST tools are unavailable вЂ” extraction will use source reading (T1-low). Run [SF] Setup Forge to detect and configure AST tools for T1 confidence."
   110в†’
   111в†’Degrade to Quick tier extraction. Note the degradation reason in context for the evidence report.
   112в†’
   113в†’**For each file вЂ” handle failures gracefully:**
   114в†’
   115в†’- If a file cannot be read: log warning, skip file, continue with remaining files
   116в†’- If AST parsing fails on a file: fall back to source reading for that file, continue
   117в†’
   118в†’**Re-export tracing (Forge/Deep only):**
   119в†’
   120в†’After the initial AST scan, check for public exports from the entry point (`__init__.py`, `index.ts`, `lib.rs`) that were NOT found by AST extraction. These are likely module-level re-exports. Follow the **Re-Export Tracing** protocol in `{extractionPatternsData}` to resolve them to their actual definition files. This ensures module re-export patterns (common in Python libraries with clean public APIs) do not create gaps in the extraction inventory.
   121в†’
   122в†’### 4b. Validate Exports Against Package Entry Point
   123в†’
   124в†’After extraction, validate the collected exports against the package's actual public API surface:
   125в†’
   126в†’- **Python:** Read `{source_root}/__init__.py` вЂ” extract all import statements to build the actual public export list. Compare against AST-extracted exports:
   127в†’  - In AST results but not in `__init__.py` в†’ mark as internal (exclude from `metadata.json` exports, keep in provenance-map with a note)
   128в†’  - In `__init__.py` but not in AST results в†’ flag as extraction gap (add to inventory, trace via re-export protocol)
   129в†’- **TypeScript/JavaScript:** Read `index.ts`/`index.js` вЂ” extract named exports. Same comparison logic.
   130в†’- **Rust:** Read `lib.rs` вЂ” extract `pub use` items. Same comparison logic.
   131в†’- **Go:** Scan package-level files for exported (capitalized) identifiers.
   132в†’
   133в†’Use the entry point as the authoritative source for `metadata.json`'s `exports[]` array. This prevents internal symbols from inflating the exports list and ensures all public API items are captured.
   134в†’
   135в†’**If entry point is missing or unreadable:** Skip validation with a warning. Use the AST-extracted list as-is.
   136в†’
   137в†’### 5. Build Extraction Inventory
   138в†’
   139в†’Compile all extracted data into a structured inventory:
   140в†’
   141в†’**Per-export entry:**
   142в†’- Function/type name
   143в†’- Full signature with types
   144в†’- Parameters (name, type, required/optional)
   145в†’- Return type
   146в†’- Source file and line number
   147в†’- Provenance citation (`[AST:...]` or `[SRC:...]`)
   148в†’- Confidence tier (T1 or T1-low)
   149в†’
   150в†’**Aggregate counts:**
   151в†’- Total files scanned
   152в†’- Total exports found
   153в†’- Exports by type (functions, types/interfaces, constants)
   154в†’- Confidence breakdown (T1 count, T1-low count)
   155в†’- `top_exports[]` вЂ” sorted list of the top 10-20 public API function names by prominence (import frequency or documentation position). This named field is consumed by step-03b for targeted temporal fetching and cache fingerprinting.
   156в†’
   157в†’**Co-import patterns (Forge/Deep only):**
   158в†’- Libraries commonly imported alongside extracted exports
   159в†’- Integration point suggestions
   160в†’
   161в†’### 6. Present Extraction Summary (Gate 2)
   162в†’
   163в†’Display the extraction findings for user confirmation:
   164в†’
   165в†’"**Extraction complete.**
   166в†’
   167в†’**Files scanned:** {file_count}
   168в†’**Exports found:** {export_count} ({function_count} functions, {type_count} types, {constant_count} constants)
   169в†’**Confidence:** {t1_count} T1 (AST-verified), {t1_low_count} T1-low (source reading)
   170в†’**Tier used:** {tier}
   171в†’**Co-import patterns:** {pattern_count} detected
   172в†’
   173в†’**Top exports:**
   174в†’{list top 10 exports with signatures}
   175в†’
   176в†’{warnings if any files skipped or degraded}
   177в†’
   178в†’Review the extraction summary above. Select an option to continue."
   179в†’
   180в†’### 7. Present MENU OPTIONS
   181в†’
   182в†’Display: "**Extraction Summary вЂ” Select an Option:** [C] Continue to compilation"
   183в†’
   184в†’#### EXECUTION RULES:
   185в†’
   186в†’- ALWAYS halt and wait for user input after presenting the extraction summary
   187в†’- This is Gate 2 вЂ” user must confirm before compilation proceeds
   188в†’- User may ask questions about the extraction results before continuing
   189в†’
   190в†’#### Menu Handling Logic:
   191в†’
   192в†’- IF C: Confirm extraction inventory is complete. Immediately load, read entire file, then execute `{nextStepFile}`
   193в†’- IF Any other comments or queries: answer questions about the extraction results, then redisplay the menu
   194в†’
   195в†’## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   196в†’
   197в†’ONLY WHEN the extraction inventory is built with provenance citations and the user has confirmed the extraction summary will you proceed to load `{nextStepFile}` for temporal context fetching.
   198в†’
   199в†’---
   200в†’
   201в†’## рџљЁ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   202в†’
   203в†’### вњ… SUCCESS:
   204в†’
   205в†’- Extraction patterns loaded and tier-appropriate strategy applied
   206в†’- Scope filters applied from brief
   207в†’- All accessible source files in scope scanned
   208в†’- Every extracted item has a provenance citation with confidence tier
   209в†’- Extraction inventory built with aggregate counts
   210в†’- Graceful degradation if AST tools unavailable
   211в†’- Gate 2 summary presented with export counts and confidence breakdown
   212в†’- User confirmed before proceeding
   213в†’
   214в†’### вќЊ SYSTEM FAILURE:
   215в†’
   216в†’- Including exports without provenance citations
   217в†’- Guessing or hallucinating function signatures not in source
   218в†’- Not applying scope/include/exclude filters from the brief
   219в†’- Halting on individual file read failures instead of skipping
   220в†’- Not presenting Gate 2 summary for user confirmation
   221в†’- Beginning compilation or SKILL.md assembly in this step
   222в†’- Not reporting confidence tier breakdown
   223в†’
   224в†’**Master Rule:** Zero hallucination вЂ” every extraction must trace to source code. Uncitable content is excluded, not guessed.
   225в†’

1в†’---
     2в†’name: 'step-06-validate'
     3в†’description: 'Validate compiled skill content against agentskills.io spec via skill-check'
     4в†’nextStepFile: './step-07-generate-artifacts.md'
     5в†’---
     6в†’
     7в†’# Step 6: Validate
     8в†’
     9в†’## STEP GOAL:
    10в†’
    11в†’To validate the compiled SKILL.md content against the agentskills.io specification using skill-check, auto-fix any validation failures, and confirm spec compliance before artifact generation.
    12в†’
    13в†’## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    14в†’
    15в†’### Universal Rules:
    16в†’
    17в†’- рџ“– CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    18в†’- рџЋЇ ALWAYS follow the exact instructions in the step file
    19в†’- вљ™пёЏ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    20в†’- вњ… YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    21в†’
    22в†’### Role Reinforcement:
    23в†’
    24в†’- вњ… You are a skill compilation engine performing quality assurance
    25в†’- вњ… Validation ensures spec compliance вЂ” it does not modify content semantics
    26в†’- вњ… Tool unavailability means skip validation, not halt workflow
    27в†’
    28в†’### Step-Specific Rules:
    29в†’
    30в†’- рџЋЇ Focus ONLY on validating compiled content against spec
    31в†’- рџљ« FORBIDDEN to add new content вЂ” only fix spec compliance issues
    32в†’- рџ’ѕ Validation and auto-fix modify files in the staging directory (`_bmad-output/{name}/`)
    33в†’- рџ’¬ If auto-fix fails, report issues clearly but proceed (warn, don't halt)
    34в†’- вљ™пёЏ If skill-check unavailable: skip validation, add warning to evidence report
    35в†’- вљ пёЏ Ignore non-zero exit codes from `skill-check` if the JSON output shows 0 errors вЂ” parse JSON output, not exit codes
    36в†’
    37в†’## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    38в†’
    39в†’- рџЋЇ Follow MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    40в†’- рџ’ѕ Validation results are added to evidence-report content in context
    41в†’- рџ“– Auto-fix pattern: validate в†’ fix в†’ re-validate (once)
    42в†’- рџљ« Maximum one auto-fix attempt per validation failure
    43в†’
    44в†’## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    45в†’
    46в†’- Available: All compiled content from step-05 (SKILL.md, metadata.json, etc.)
    47в†’- Focus: Spec compliance validation and auto-fix
    48в†’- Limits: Do NOT add new content or modify extraction data
    49в†’- Dependencies: Compiled content must exist from step-05
    50в†’
    51в†’## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    52в†’
    53в†’**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    54в†’
    55в†’### 1. Check Tool Availability
    56в†’
    57в†’Run: `npx skill-check -h`
    58в†’
    59в†’- If succeeds: Continue to automated validation (section 2)
    60в†’- If fails: Perform manual fallback (section 3); add note to evidence-report: "Spec validation performed manually вЂ” skill-check tool unavailable"
    61в†’
    62в†’**Important:** Do not assume availability вЂ” empirical check required.
    63в†’
    64в†’### 2. Validate & Auto-Fix (skill-check check --fix)
    65в†’
    66в†’Run the external skill-check tool against the compiled skill staging directory:
    67в†’
    68в†’```bash
    69в†’npx skill-check check <staging-skill-dir> --fix --format json --no-security-scan
    70в†’```
    71в†’
    72в†’This performs frontmatter validation, description quality checks, body limit enforcement, local link resolution, file formatting, auto-fix of deterministic issues, and quality scoring (0-100) across five weighted categories.
    73в†’
    74в†’**Parse the JSON output** for: `qualityScore` (0-100), `diagnostics[]` (remaining issues), `fixed[]` (auto-corrected issues).
    75в†’
    76в†’**Note:** `skill-check` may return non-zero exit code even when `errorCount` is 0. Always rely on parsed JSON, not the shell exit code.
    77в†’
    78в†’- **Score в‰Ґ 70:** Record "Schema: PASS (score: {score}/100)" in evidence-report
    79в†’- **Score < 70:** Log remaining diagnostics as warnings, record "Schema: WARN вЂ” score {score}/100, {count} remaining issues", proceed
    80в†’- **Unfixable errors:** Record specific rule IDs and suggestions, proceed with warnings
    81в†’
    82в†’### 3. Validate Frontmatter (Fallback)
    83в†’
    84в†’**If skill-check was available:** Skip вЂ” already validated in step 2.
    85в†’
    86в†’**If skill-check NOT available (fallback):** Perform manual frontmatter compliance check:
    87в†’
    88в†’- [ ] Frontmatter present вЂ” file starts with `---` and has closing `---`
    89в†’- [ ] `name` field вЂ” present, non-empty, lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens only, 1-64 chars
    90в†’- [ ] `name` matches skill output directory name
    91в†’- [ ] `description` field вЂ” present, non-empty, 1-1024 characters
    92в†’- [ ] No unknown fields вЂ” only `name`, `description`, `license`, `compatibility`, `metadata`, `allowed-tools` permitted
    93в†’- [ ] `version` and `author` are NOT in frontmatter (they belong in metadata.json)
    94в†’
    95в†’If fails: auto-fix (deterministic), re-validate once, record result. If passes: record "Frontmatter: PASS".
    96в†’
    97в†’### 4. Split Oversized Body (if needed)
    98в†’
    99в†’**If step 2 reported `body.max_lines` failure:**
   100в†’
   101в†’```bash
   102в†’npx skill-check split-body <staging-skill-dir> --write
   103в†’```
   104в†’
   105в†’Then re-validate: `npx skill-check check <staging-skill-dir> --format json --no-security-scan`
   106в†’
   107в†’**If skill-check unavailable or no body size issue:** Skip.
   108в†’
   109в†’### 5. Security Scan
   110в†’
   111в†’**If skill-check available:**
   112в†’
   113в†’```bash
   114в†’npx skill-check check <staging-skill-dir> --format json
   115в†’```
   116в†’
   117в†’(Security scan enabled by default when `--no-security-scan` omitted. The scan uses [Snyk Agent Scan](https://github.com/snyk/agent-scan) to check for prompt injection risks, sensitive data exposure, and unsafe tool permissions.)
   118в†’
   119в†’Record any security warnings in evidence-report. Security findings are advisory вЂ” they do not block artifact generation.
   120в†’
   121в†’**If security scan fails due to missing SNYK_TOKEN:**
   122в†’
   123в†’Display actionable guidance:
   124в†’
   125в†’"Security scan requires a Snyk API token. **Important:** The Snyk API requires an Enterprise plan ([authentication docs](https://docs.snyk.io/snyk-api/authentication-for-api)). To enable:
   126в†’1. Obtain a Snyk Enterprise account with API access
   127в†’2. Get your API token from Account Settings в†’ API Token
   128в†’3. Set `SNYK_TOKEN=your-token` in your environment or `.env`
   129в†’4. Re-run [SF] Setup Forge to update tool detection, then re-run [CS] Create Skill
   130в†’
   131в†’If you don't have an Enterprise account, use `--no-security-scan` flag in skill-check to skip, or `--security-scan-runner pipx|uvx|local` to control the execution method. Security scanning is optional and does not affect tier level or block skill compilation."
   132в†’
   133в†’Record: "Security scan skipped вЂ” SNYK_TOKEN not configured"
   134в†’
   135в†’**If skill-check unavailable:** Skip with note: "Security scan skipped вЂ” skill-check tool unavailable"
   136в†’
   137в†’### 6. Content Quality Review (tessl)
   138в†’
   139в†’**If tessl available**, run: `npx -y tessl skill review <staging-skill-dir>`
   140в†’
   141в†’Parse output for: `description_score`, `content_score`, `average_score`, `validation_result`, `judge_suggestions[]`.
   142в†’
   143в†’- **Content score < 70%:** Record warning: "Content quality warning: tessl scored content at {score}%."
   144в†’- **Unavailable:** Skip with note: "Content quality review skipped вЂ” tessl tool unavailable"
   145в†’
   146в†’**Expected scoring for two-tier design:** The Skill Forge two-tier design (Tier 1 Key API Summary + Tier 2 Full API Reference) intentionally includes progressive disclosure. tessl's `conciseness` scorer may flag this as redundancy (typically scoring 2/3), which is expected behavior вЂ” not a defect. Content scores >= 60% are acceptable when both tiers are correctly applied with proper differentiation (see step-05 Tier 2 assembly rules). Do NOT consolidate Tier 1 and Tier 2 content to improve the score вЂ” the two-tier structure is a deliberate design choice for standalone usability.
   147в†’
   148в†’tessl installs automatically via `npx`. A missing tool is not an error вЂ” graceful skip.
   149в†’
   150в†’#### 6b. User Decision Gate (conditional)
   151в†’
   152в†’**If tessl returned no suggestions OR tessl was unavailable:** Skip this gate вЂ” auto-proceed.
   153в†’
   154в†’**If tessl returned suggestions**, present them to the user:
   155в†’
   156в†’"**Content quality review: {score}%**
   157в†’
   158в†’tessl suggestions:
   159в†’{numbered list of judge_suggestions}
   160в†’
   161в†’**Select an option:**
   162в†’- **[S] Skip** вЂ” proceed with current content as-is (default)
   163в†’- **[A] Apply structural fixes** вЂ” apply only structural suggestions (split sections, consolidate duplicates). No new content generated.
   164в†’- **[R] Review all** вЂ” show each suggestion with proposed changes before applying"
   165в†’
   166в†’#### Gate Rules:
   167в†’
   168в†’- **Structural suggestions** (split reference section, consolidate duplicates, reorder sections) can be applied without zero-hallucination risk вЂ” they restructure existing content
   169в†’- **Semantic suggestions** (add examples, add error handling, add validation checkpoints) introduce content not verified from source code. If the user chooses to apply these:
   170в†’  - Warn: "This adds content not verified from source code."
   171в†’  - Mark applied content with `<!-- [TESSL:auto-fix] -->` markers
   172в†’  - Cite as `[TESSL:suggestion]` in the provenance map with `confidence: "TESSL"` (below T3)
   173в†’  - Record in evidence report: "TESSL-suggested content applied: {count} items (unverified)"
   174в†’- **If user selects [S]:** Record "tessl suggestions: skipped by user" in evidence report. Proceed to section 7.
   175в†’- **If user selects [A]:** Apply structural fixes only, re-run tessl to capture updated score, record results. Proceed to section 7.
   176в†’- **If user selects [R]:** Show each suggestion with the proposed change. For each, user confirms or skips. Apply confirmed changes, record results. Proceed to section 7.
   177в†’
   178в†’### 7. Validate metadata.json
   179в†’
   180в†’Cross-check metadata.json against extraction inventory:
   181в†’- `stats.exports_documented` / `stats.exports_public_api` / `stats.exports_internal` / `stats.exports_total` are accurate
   182в†’- `stats.public_api_coverage` and `stats.total_coverage` are correctly computed (null when denominator is 0)
   183в†’- `confidence_t1`, `confidence_t2`, `confidence_t3` match actual counts
   184в†’- `spec_version` is "1.3"
   185в†’
   186в†’Auto-fix any discrepancies (these are computed values).
   187в†’
   188в†’### 8. Update Evidence Report
   189в†’
   190в†’Add validation results to evidence-report content in context:
   191в†’
   192в†’```markdown
   193в†’## Validation Results
   194в†’- Schema: {pass/fail} (quality score: {score}/100)
   195в†’- Frontmatter: {pass/fail}
   196в†’- Body: {pass/fail} {split-body applied if applicable}
   197в†’- Security: {pass/warn/skipped}
   198в†’- Content Quality (tessl): {pass/warn/skipped} (score: {score}%)
   199в†’- Metadata: {pass/fail}
   200в†’
   201в†’## Quality Score Breakdown
   202в†’- Frontmatter (30%): {score} | Description (30%): {score} | Body (20%): {score} | Links (10%): {score} | File (10%): {score}
   203в†’
   204в†’## Auto-Fixed Issues
   205в†’- {list of issues automatically corrected by --fix}
   206в†’
   207в†’## Remaining Warnings / Security Findings / Content Quality (tessl)
   208в†’- {warnings, security results, tessl scores and suggestions вЂ” or "skipped"}
   209в†’```
   210в†’
   211в†’### 9. Menu Handling Logic
   212в†’
   213в†’**Conditional interaction step.** If tessl produced suggestions, section 6b halts for user input. Otherwise, auto-proceed.
   214в†’
   215в†’After validation completes (including any user decisions from section 6b), immediately load, read entire file, then execute `{nextStepFile}`.
   216в†’
   217в†’- Tool unavailability is a skip, not a halt
   218в†’- Validation failures are warnings вЂ” proceed to artifact generation
   219в†’- tessl gate only triggers when suggestions exist вЂ” no gate for clean reviews or unavailable tools
   220в†’
   221в†’## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   222в†’
   223в†’ONLY WHEN validation is complete (or skipped) and evidence-report content is updated will you proceed to load `{nextStepFile}` for artifact generation.
   224в†’
   225в†’---
   226в†’
   227в†’## рџљЁ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   228в†’
   229в†’### вњ… SUCCESS:
   230в†’
   231в†’- `npx skill-check check --fix --format json` executed (or skipped with warning if unavailable)
   232в†’- Quality score captured and recorded; auto-fix applied for deterministic issues
   233в†’- Split-body applied if `body.max_lines` failed; security scan executed (or skipped with warning)
   234в†’- `npx -y tessl skill review` executed (or skipped); content quality warning raised if score < 70%
   235в†’- tessl suggestions presented to user when available; user decision recorded
   236в†’- TESSL-applied content marked with `<!-- [TESSL:auto-fix] -->` and cited as `[TESSL:suggestion]`
   237в†’- Metadata cross-check performed; evidence report updated with structured results
   238в†’- Proceeded to step-07 (auto or after user gate)
   239в†’
   240в†’### вќЊ SYSTEM FAILURE:
   241в†’
   242в†’- Halting on validation failure or skill-check unavailability (should warn and proceed)
   243в†’- Adding new content during validation without user approval via the tessl gate
   244в†’- Applying semantic tessl suggestions without warning the user about unverified content
   245в†’- Not recording quality score; skipping security scan without recording the skip
   246в†’- Attempting more than one auto-fix cycle per failure
   247в†’
   248в†’**Master Rule:** Validation informs, it does not block. Record results, fix what's deterministic, scan for security issues, warn about the rest, and proceed.
   249в†’

1в†’# Extraction Patterns by Tier
     2в†’
     3в†’## Quick Tier (No AST)
     4в†’
     5в†’Source reading via gh_bridge вЂ” infer exports from file structure and content.
     6в†’
     7в†’### Strategy
     8в†’1. `gh_bridge.list_tree(owner, repo, branch)` вЂ” map source structure
     9в†’2. Identify entry points: index files, main exports, public modules
    10в†’3. `gh_bridge.read_file(owner, repo, path)` вЂ” read each entry point
    11в†’4. Extract: exported function names, parameter lists, return types (from signatures)
    12в†’5. Infer types from JSDoc, docstrings, type annotations in source
    13в†’
    14в†’### Confidence
    15в†’- All results: T1-low (source reading without structural verification)
    16в†’- No co-import detection available
    17в†’- No AST-backed line numbers
    18в†’
    19в†’### Supported Patterns
    20в†’- `export function name(...)` / `export const name = ...` (JS/TS)
    21в†’- `pub fn name(...)` (Rust)
    22в†’- `def name(...)` with `__all__` (Python)
    23в†’- `func Name(...)` (Go, capitalized = exported)
    24в†’
    25в†’---
    26в†’
    27в†’## Forge Tier (AST Available)
    28в†’
    29в†’Structural extraction via ast_bridge вЂ” verified exports with line-level citations.
    30в†’
    31в†’### Strategy
    32в†’1. Detect language from brief or file extensions
    33в†’2. `ast_bridge.scan_definitions(path, language)` вЂ” extract all exports
    34в†’3. For each export: function name, full signature, parameter types, return type, line number
    35в†’4. `ast_bridge.detect_co_imports(path, libraries[])` вЂ” find integration points
    36в†’5. Build extraction rules YAML for reproducibility
    37в†’
    38в†’### Confidence
    39в†’- Exported functions with full signatures: T1 (AST-verified)
    40в†’- Type definitions and interfaces: T1
    41в†’- Co-import patterns: T1
    42в†’- Internal/private functions: excluded (not part of public API)
    43в†’
    44в†’### ast-grep Patterns
    45в†’- JS/TS: `export function $NAME($$$PARAMS): $RET` / `export const $NAME`
    46в†’- Rust: `pub fn $NAME($$$PARAMS) -> $RET`
    47в†’- Python: function definitions within `__all__` list
    48в†’- Go: capitalized function definitions
    49в†’
    50в†’---
    51в†’
    52в†’## Deep Tier (AST + QMD)
    53в†’
    54в†’Same extraction as Forge tier. Deep tier adds enrichment in step-04, not extraction.
    55в†’
    56в†’### Strategy
    57в†’- Identical to Forge tier extraction
    58в†’- QMD enrichment happens in the next step (step-04-enrich)
    59в†’- Extraction results carry forward unchanged
    60в†’
    61в†’### Confidence
    62в†’- Extraction: same as Forge (T1)
    63в†’- Enrichment annotations added in step-04: T2
    64в†’
    65в†’---
    66в†’
    67в†’## AST Extraction Protocol
    68в†’
    69в†’When AST tools are available (Forge/Deep tier), follow this deterministic protocol to prevent output overflow on large codebases.
    70в†’
    71в†’**"Files in scope"** = files remaining after applying `include_patterns` and `exclude_patterns` from the brief, filtered by the target language extension. This is NOT the total repository file count from step-01's tree listing. Use the filtered count from step-03 section 2 as the decision tree input.
    72в†’
    73в†’### Decision Tree
    74в†’
    75в†’Apply the first matching condition:
    76в†’
    77в†’```
    78в†’Files in scope в‰¤ 100
    79в†’  в†’ Use ast-grep MCP tool: find_code(pattern, max_results=100, output_format="text")
    80в†’  в†’ Parse compact text output directly into extraction inventory
    81в†’
    82в†’Files in scope 101вЂ“500
    83в†’  в†’ Use ast-grep MCP tool: find_code_by_rule(yaml, max_results=150, output_format="text")
    84в†’  в†’ Use scoped YAML rules (see recipes below) to filter at the AST level
    85в†’  в†’ Parse compact text output into extraction inventory
    86в†’
    87в†’Files in scope > 500
    88в†’  в†’ CLI streaming fallback: ast-grep --json=stream + line-by-line Python processing
    89в†’  в†’ Process in directory batches, cap per-batch output
    90в†’  в†’ Merge batch results into extraction inventory
    91в†’```
    92в†’
    93в†’### Safety Valve
    94в†’
    95в†’If any ast-grep operation (MCP or CLI) visibly causes a timeout, returns an error related to output size, or produces unexpectedly large output: immediately switch to the CLI streaming fallback with `--json=stream`. Do not retry the same approach. When falling back to the CLI streaming template, inject the brief's `scope.exclude` patterns into the `EXCLUDES` list (use `[]` if absent) вЂ” this applies regardless of which path triggered the fallback. Note: `max_results` in the MCP tool and `| head -N` in the CLI path provide hard caps, but this safety valve covers cases where the upstream tool itself fails before returning results (e.g., OOM during JSON serialization).
    96в†’
    97в†’### MCP Tool Usage (Preferred)
    98в†’
    99в†’**Simple pattern search:**
   100в†’
   101в†’```
   102в†’find_code(
   103в†’  project_folder="{source_path}",
   104в†’  pattern="async def $NAME($$$PARAMS)",
   105в†’  language="python",
   106в†’  max_results=100,
   107в†’  output_format="text"
   108в†’)
   109в†’```
   110в†’
   111в†’**Scoped YAML rule search (for larger repos):**
   112в†’
   113в†’```
   114в†’find_code_by_rule(
   115в†’  project_folder="{source_path}",
   116в†’  yaml="id: public-api\nlanguage: python\nrule:\n  pattern: 'def $NAME($$$PARAMS)'\n  inside:\n    kind: module\n    stopBy: end\nconstraints:\n  NAME:\n    regex: '^[^_]'",
   117в†’  max_results=150,
   118в†’  output_format="text"
   119в†’)
   120в†’```
   121в†’
   122в†’### CLI Streaming Fallback
   123в†’
   124в†’When MCP tools are unavailable or the repo exceeds 500 files in scope, use `--json=stream` (NEVER `--json` or `--json=pretty`) with line-by-line Python processing:
   125в†’
   126в†’```bash
   127в†’# Note: use $$$ for variadic params in ast-grep patterns (e.g., 'def $NAME($$$PARAMS)')
   128в†’# {exclude_patterns} = Python list from brief's scope.exclude, e.g. ['tests/**', '**/test_*']
   129в†’# If scope.exclude is absent or empty in the brief, inject [] as the default.
   130в†’# Patterns are matched against the full file path as emitted by ast-grep.
   131в†’# Ensure paths are relative to the same root as the patterns (strip ./ prefix if needed).
   132в†’ast-grep -p '{pattern}' -l {language} --json=stream {path} | python3 -c "
   133в†’import sys, json, fnmatch
   134в†’
   135в†’EXCLUDES = {exclude_patterns}
   136в†’
   137в†’for line in sys.stdin:
   138в†’    try:
   139в†’        m = json.loads(line)
   140в†’        f = m.get('file','')
   141в†’        if EXCLUDES and any(fnmatch.fnmatch(f, pat) for pat in EXCLUDES):
   142в†’            continue
   143в†’        v = m.get('metaVariables',{})
   144в†’        name = v.get('single',{}).get('NAME',{}).get('text','')
   145в†’        if name and not name.startswith('_'):
   146в†’            ln = m.get('range',{}).get('start',{}).get('line',0)+1
   147в†’            sig = m.get('text','').split(chr(10))[0].strip()
   148в†’            print(f'[AST:{f}:L{ln}] {sig}')
   149в†’    except: pass
   150в†’" | head -200
   151в†’```
   152в†’
   153в†’**Critical constraints:**
   154в†’
   155в†’- ALWAYS use `--json=stream` вЂ” never `--json` (loads entire array into memory)
   156в†’- ALWAYS process line-by-line (`for line in sys.stdin`) вЂ” never `json.load(sys.stdin)`
   157в†’- ALWAYS cap output with `| head -N` as a safety valve
   158в†’- For repos > 500 files, process in directory batches of 20-50 files each
   159в†’
   160в†’### YAML Rule Recipes by Language
   161в†’
   162в†’**Python вЂ” public functions:**
   163в†’
   164в†’```yaml
   165в†’id: python-public-functions
   166в†’language: python
   167в†’rule:
   168в†’  pattern: 'def $NAME($$$PARAMS)'
   169в†’  inside:
   170в†’    kind: module
   171в†’    stopBy: end
   172в†’constraints:
   173в†’  NAME:
   174в†’    regex: '^[^_]'
   175в†’```
   176в†’
   177в†’**Python вЂ” public classes:**
   178в†’
   179в†’```yaml
   180в†’id: python-public-classes
   181в†’language: python
   182в†’rule:
   183в†’  pattern: 'class $NAME($$$BASES)'
   184в†’  inside:
   185в†’    kind: module
   186в†’    stopBy: end
   187в†’constraints:
   188в†’  NAME:
   189в†’    regex: '^[^_]'
   190в†’```
   191в†’
   192в†’**JavaScript/TypeScript вЂ” exported functions:**
   193в†’
   194в†’```yaml
   195в†’id: js-exported-functions
   196в†’language: typescript
   197в†’rule:
   198в†’  pattern: 'export function $NAME($$$PARAMS)'
   199в†’```
   200в†’
   201в†’**JavaScript/TypeScript вЂ” exported constants:**
   202в†’
   203в†’```yaml
   204в†’id: js-exported-constants
   205в†’language: typescript
   206в†’rule:
   207в†’  pattern: 'export const $NAME = $VALUE'
   208в†’```
   209в†’
   210в†’**Rust вЂ” public functions:**
   211в†’
   212в†’```yaml
   213в†’id: rust-public-functions
   214в†’language: rust
   215в†’rule:
   216в†’  any:
   217в†’    - pattern: 'pub fn $NAME($$$PARAMS) -> $RET'
   218в†’    - pattern: 'pub fn $NAME($$$PARAMS)'
   219в†’```
   220в†’
   221в†’**Go вЂ” exported functions (capitalized):**
   222в†’
   223в†’```yaml
   224в†’id: go-exported-functions
   225в†’language: go
   226в†’rule:
   227в†’  pattern: 'func $NAME($$$PARAMS) $RET'
   228в†’constraints:
   229в†’  NAME:
   230в†’    regex: '^[A-Z]'
   231в†’```
   232в†’
   233в†’### Re-Export Tracing
   234в†’
   235в†’After initial AST extraction, some top-level exports may resolve to **module imports** rather than direct function definitions. This is common in Python libraries that use `__init__.py` re-exports for a clean public API.
   236в†’
   237в†’**Detection heuristic:** For each top-level export from `__init__.py` (or equivalent entry point), check if the import path resolves to a directory (contains `__init__.py`) rather than a `.py` file with a matching `def` or `class`. If the initial AST scan found no function/class definition for a known public export, it is likely a module re-export.
   238в†’
   239в†’**Tracing protocol:**
   240в†’
   241в†’1. Read the entry point file (e.g., `{package}/__init__.py`) and extract all `from .X import Y` statements
   242в†’2. For each import where Y was NOT found by the initial AST scan:
   243в†’   - Check if the import path resolves to a directory (e.g., `{package}/api/v1/delete/` exists with `__init__.py`)
   244в†’   - If directory: read its `__init__.py` to find the actual re-exported symbol
   245в†’   - Trace the symbol to its definition file and run AST extraction on that file
   246в†’3. Cite the actual definition location: `[AST:{definition_file}:L{line}]`
   247в†’
   248в†’**Examples:**
   249в†’
   250в†’```python
   251в†’# Module re-export вЂ” follow required
   252в†’from .api.v1.delete import delete    # delete/ is a directory в†’ read delete/__init__.py
   253в†’
   254в†’# Direct function import вЂ” no follow needed
   255в†’from .api.v1.add.add import add      # add.py exists with def add()
   256в†’```
   257в†’
   258в†’**Unresolvable imports:** If the import statement is a star-import (`from .X import *`) or a conditional import (`try`/`except`), the symbol cannot be reliably traced via this protocol. Record it with `[SRC:{package}/__init__.py:L{line}]` (T1-low) and a note: "star/conditional import вЂ” manual trace required."
   259в†’
   260в†’**Scope limit:** Only trace re-exports for symbols listed in the top-level entry point's public API. Do not recursively trace beyond one level of `__init__.py` indirection. If a re-export cannot be resolved after one level, record it with a `[SRC:{package}/__init__.py:L{line}]` citation (T1-low) from the import statement itself.
   261в†’
   262в†’**Other languages:** JS/TS barrel files (`index.ts` with `export { X } from './module'`) follow the same principle вЂ” trace the re-export to the definition file. Rust `pub use` and Go package-level re-exports are less common but follow the same heuristic when encountered.
   263в†’
   264в†’---
   265в†’
   266в†’## Tier Degradation Rules
   267в†’
   268в†’### Remote Source at Forge/Deep Tier
   269в†’
   270в†’When `source_repo` is a remote URL (GitHub URL or owner/repo format) and the tier is Forge or Deep:
   271в†’
   272в†’- **ast-grep requires local files** вЂ” it cannot operate on remote URLs
   273в†’
   274в†’**Ephemeral clone strategy (preferred):**
   275в†’
   276в†’1. Check `git` availability (`git --version`). `git` is effectively guaranteed at Deep tier (via `gh` dependency) but NOT guaranteed at Forge tier.
   277в†’2. If `git` is available: perform an ephemeral shallow clone to a system temp path (`{system_temp}/skf-ephemeral-{skill-name}-{timestamp}/`).
   278в†’3. For create-skill: use `--depth 1 --single-branch --filter=blob:none`; if `include_patterns` are specified, apply mode selection: if `exclude_patterns` are absent, use cone mode (convert include_patterns to directory roots; use `--skip-checks` for individual file paths); if `exclude_patterns` are present, use `--no-cone` mode (pass gitignore-style patterns with `!`-prefixed excludes вЂ” includes first, then negations). See source-resolution-protocols.md for the full conversion rules.
   279в†’4. For update-skill: use sparse-checkout with `--skip-checks` scoped to the changed files from the change manifest only (file paths require `--skip-checks`). No `--branch` flag вЂ” uses the remote default branch (must match the branch used during original create-skill run).
   280в†’5. If clone succeeds: use the local clone path for AST extraction. All results are T1 with `[AST:...]` citations.
   281в†’6. Cleanup: delete the temp directory after extraction inventory is built and all data is in context. The clone never persists beyond the extraction step.
   282в†’
   283в†’**Fallback (clone fails or `git` unavailable):**
   284в†’
   285в†’- The extraction step MUST warn the user explicitly before degrading
   286в†’- **create-skill:** Warning must include actionable guidance вЂ” clone locally and update `source_repo` in the brief to the local path
   287в†’- **update-skill:** Warning must include actionable guidance вЂ” clone locally, re-run [CS] Create Skill with the local path to regenerate provenance data, then re-run the update
   288в†’- Extraction proceeds using Quick tier strategy (source reading via gh_bridge)
   289в†’- All results labeled T1-low with `[SRC:...]` citations
   290в†’- The degradation reason is recorded in the evidence report
   291в†’
   292в†’Silent degradation is **forbidden**. The user must always know when AST extraction was skipped and why.
   293в†’
   294в†’### AST Tool Unavailable at Forge/Deep Tier
   295в†’
   296в†’When the tier is Forge or Deep but ast-grep is not functional:
   297в†’
   298в†’- The extraction step MUST warn the user explicitly before degrading
   299в†’- Warning must include actionable guidance: run [SF] Setup Forge to detect tools
   300в†’- Extraction proceeds using Quick tier strategy
   301в†’- All results labeled T1-low
   302в†’- The degradation reason is recorded in the evidence report
   303в†’
   304в†’### Per-File AST Failure
   305в†’
   306в†’When ast-grep fails on an individual file (parse error, unsupported syntax):
   307в†’
   308в†’- Fall back to source reading for **that file only**
   309в†’- Other files continue with AST extraction
   310в†’- The affected file's results are labeled T1-low; unaffected files retain T1
   311в†’- Log a warning noting which file degraded and why
   312в†’

1в†’# Source Access Protocol
     2в†’
     3в†’## Source API Surface Definition
     4в†’
     5в†’**Source API surface** = the package's top-level public exports. These are the symbols reachable from the primary entry point without importing internal modules:
     6в†’
     7в†’- **Python:** symbols exported in `__init__.py` (including re-exports) вЂ” exclude private (`_prefixed`) names
     8в†’- **TypeScript/JavaScript:** named exports from `index.ts` / `index.js` вЂ” exclude unexported locals
     9в†’- **Go:** exported identifiers (capitalized) from the package's public-facing files
    10в†’- **Rust:** items in `pub use` from `lib.rs` or `mod.rs`
    11в†’
    12в†’Internal module symbols are **excluded** from the coverage denominator unless they are explicitly documented in SKILL.md (in which case they count as documented extras, not missing coverage).
    13в†’
    14в†’This matches the extraction-patterns.md convention used during skill creation: coverage measures how well SKILL.md documents what users actually import, not the entire internal codebase.
    15в†’
    16в†’## Source Access Resolution
    17в†’
    18в†’Before analysis, determine source access level. Walk through these states in order вЂ” use the first that succeeds:
    19в†’
    20в†’**State 1 вЂ” Local source available:**
    21в†’Check if `{source_path}` (from metadata.json `source_root`) exists on disk. If yes в†’ full analysis at detected tier (AST + signatures). Set `analysis_confidence: full`.
    22в†’
    23в†’**State 2 вЂ” Local absent, provenance-map exists:**
    24в†’Check `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/provenance-map.json`. If present, use it as the baseline export inventory вЂ” each entry contains structured fields: `export_name`, `export_type`, `params[]`, `return_type`, `source_file`, `source_line`, `confidence`, and `ast_node_type`. Cross-reference against SKILL.md documented exports for name-matching and param-by-param coverage. Signature verification compares SKILL.md's documented params/return types against provenance-map entries directly. If remote reading tools are available (zread, deepwiki, gh API, or similar), supplement by reading the entry point file for live signature verification. Set `analysis_confidence: provenance-map`.
    25в†’
    26в†’**State 3 вЂ” No provenance-map, metadata exports exist (quick-skill path):**
    27в†’If no provenance-map.json exists (typical for quick-skill output), fall back to `metadata.json`'s `exports[]` array for the export name list. Coverage check becomes a self-consistency comparison: are all names in `exports[]` documented in SKILL.md with description, parameters, and return type? Signatures cannot be verified. If remote reading tools are available, supplement by reading the entry point for live export comparison. Set `analysis_confidence: metadata-only`.
    28в†’
    29в†’**State 4 вЂ” No local source, no forge-data, remote tools available:**
    30в†’If neither provenance-map nor metadata exports provide a usable baseline, but remote reading tools (zread, deepwiki, gh API, or similar) are available and `source_repo` is set in metadata.json, read the entry point remotely to build the export inventory from scratch. Name-matching only вЂ” no AST. Set `analysis_confidence: remote-only`.
    31в†’
    32в†’**State 5 вЂ” No source access at all:**
    33в†’If none of the above succeed, fall through to docs-only mode (section 0 already handles this). Set `analysis_confidence: docs-only`. Warn: "**No source access available.** Coverage check evaluates documentation self-consistency only. Re-run with local clone or remote access for source-backed verification."
    34в†’
    35в†’Set `analysis_confidence` in context for use in Section 2 analysis depth, step-05 output, and step-05 scoring.
    36в†’
    37в†’**Confidence tier mapping:** `full` = T1, `provenance-map` = T1, `metadata-only` = T1-low, `remote-only` = T1-low, `docs-only` = T3. This aligns with the T1/T1-low/T2/T3 scale used across all SKF workflows.
    38в†’

    10в†’
    11в†’# Step 3: Coverage Check
    12в†’
    13в†’## STEP GOAL:
    14в†’
    15в†’Compare the exports, functions, classes, types, and interfaces documented in SKILL.md against the actual source code API surface. Identify missing documentation, undocumented exports, and signature mismatches. Analysis depth scales with forge tier.
    16в†’
    17в†’## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    18в†’
    19в†’### Universal Rules:
    20в†’
    21в†’- рџ›‘ NEVER fabricate findings вЂ” every coverage result must trace to actual source code or SKILL.md content
    22в†’- рџ“– CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    23в†’- рџ”„ CRITICAL: When loading next step, ensure entire file is read
    24в†’- вљ™пёЏ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    25в†’- вњ… YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT In your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    26в†’
    27в†’### Role Reinforcement:
    28в†’
    29в†’- вњ… You are a skill auditor in Ferris's Audit mode вЂ” zero hallucination
    30в†’- вњ… If you already have been given a name, communication_style and identity, continue to use those while playing this new role
    31в†’- вњ… Every finding must include file:line citations from source code
    32в†’- вњ… Report what IS documented vs what SHOULD BE documented вЂ” facts only
    33в†’
    34в†’### Step-Specific Rules:
    35в†’
    36в†’- рџЋЇ Use subprocess optimization for per-file AST analysis when available
    37в†’- рџ’¬ Subprocess returns structured findings only, not full file contents
    38в†’- рџљ« DO NOT BE LAZY вЂ” For EACH source file, launch a subprocess for deep analysis
    39в†’- вљ™пёЏ If subprocess unavailable, perform analysis in main thread sequentially
    40в†’- рџ“‹ Coverage depth must match the detected forge tier
    41в†’
    42в†’## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    43в†’
    44в†’- рџЋЇ Load SKILL.md exports section and source files
    45в†’- рџ’ѕ Append Coverage Analysis section to {outputFile}
    46в†’- рџ“– Update stepsCompleted in {outputFile}
    47в†’- рџљ« FORBIDDEN to proceed without completing all source file analysis
    48в†’
    49в†’## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    50в†’
    51в†’- Available: SKILL.md, source files, forge tier, test mode from step 02
    52в†’- Focus: Export coverage comparison only вЂ” coherence is step 04
    53в†’- Limits: Do NOT validate cross-references or integration patterns (that's coherence)
    54в†’- Dependencies: step-02 must have set testMode and reported forge tier
    55в†’
    56в†’## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    57в†’
    58в†’**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise unless user explicitly requests a change.
    59в†’
    60в†’### 0. Check for Docs-Only Mode
    61в†’
    62в†’**If metadata.json indicates `confidence_tier: "Quick"` and all SKILL.md citations are `[EXT:...]` format (docs-only skill):**
    63в†’
    64в†’Coverage scoring adapts: instead of comparing SKILL.md against source code exports, compare SKILL.md documented items against themselves for internal completeness (every documented function has a description, parameters, and return type). Score based on documentation completeness rather than source coverage. Set `docs_only_mode: true` in context for step-05 scoring.
    65в†’
    66в†’"**Docs-only skill detected.** Coverage check evaluates documentation completeness rather than source code coverage."
    67в†’
    68в†’**If source-based skill:** Continue with standard coverage check below.
    69в†’
    70в†’### 0b. Load Source Access Protocol
    71в†’
    72в†’Load `{sourceAccessProtocol}` and follow both sections:
    73в†’1. **Source API Surface Definition** вЂ” determines what counts as the public API for coverage denominator
    74в†’2. **Source Access Resolution** вЂ” 5-state waterfall to determine how source files will be read and sets `analysis_confidence`
    75в†’
    76в†’### 1. Extract Documented Exports from SKILL.md
    77в†’
    78в†’Read SKILL.md and extract all documented items:
    79в†’
    80в†’- **Functions:** name, parameters, return type, description
    81в†’- **Classes:** name, methods, properties
    82в†’- **Types/Interfaces:** name, fields
    83в†’- **Constants/Enums:** name, values
    84в†’- **Hooks/Patterns:** name, usage signature
    85в†’
    86в†’Build the **documented inventory** вЂ” a list of everything the SKILL.md claims the source provides.
    87в†’
    88в†’### 2. Analyze Source Code (Tier-Dependent)
    89в†’
    90в†’Start from the package entry point (see 0b) and identify the public API surface. Then analyze those exports at the appropriate tier depth.
    91в†’
    92в†’**Quick Tier (no tools):**
    93в†’- Read the entry point file(s) directly
    94в†’- Identify public exports by scanning for `export` keywords, `module.exports`, `__init__.py` imports, or language-specific export patterns
    95в†’- Compare against documented inventory by name matching
    96в†’- Cannot verify signatures вЂ” note as "unverified" in report
    97в†’
    98в†’**Forge Tier (ast-grep available):**
    99в†’DO NOT BE LAZY вЂ” For EACH source file that defines public API exports, launch a subprocess that:
   100в†’1. Uses ast-grep to extract all exported symbols with their full signatures
   101в†’2. Matches each export against the documented inventory
   102в†’3. Returns structured findings:
   103в†’
   104в†’```json
   105в†’{
   106в†’  "file": "src/utils.ts",
   107в†’  "exports_found": ["formatDate", "parseConfig", "ConfigType"],
   108в†’  "exports_documented": ["formatDate", "parseConfig"],
   109в†’  "missing_docs": ["ConfigType"],
   110в†’  "signature_mismatches": [
   111в†’    {
   112в†’      "name": "formatDate",
   113в†’      "source_sig": "(date: Date, format?: string) => string",
   114в†’      "documented_sig": "(date: Date) => string",
   115в†’      "issue": "missing optional parameter 'format'"
   116в†’    }
   117в†’  ]
   118в†’}
   119в†’```
   120в†’
   121в†’If subprocess unavailable, perform ast-grep analysis in main thread per file.
   122в†’
   123в†’**Deep Tier (ast-grep + gh + QMD):**
   124в†’- All Forge tier checks, plus:
   125в†’- Use gh CLI to verify source repository matches documented version
   126в†’- Cross-check type definitions against their source declarations
   127в†’- Verify re-exported symbols trace to their original source
   128в†’
   129в†’### 3. Build Coverage Results
   130в†’
   131в†’Aggregate findings across all source files:
   132в†’
   133в†’**Per-export status table:**
   134в†’
   135в†’| Export | Type | Documented | Signature Match | File:Line | Status |
   136в†’|--------|------|-----------|-----------------|-----------|--------|
   137в†’| {name} | function/class/type | yes/no | yes/no/unverified | src/file.ts:42 | PASS/FAIL/WARN |
   138в†’
   139в†’**Summary counts:**
   140в†’- Total exports in source: {N}
   141в†’- Documented in SKILL.md: {N}
   142в†’- Missing documentation: {N}
   143в†’- Signature mismatches: {N}
   144в†’- Undocumented in SKILL.md but not in source (stale docs): {N}
   145в†’
   146в†’### 4. Load Scoring Rules
   147в†’
   148в†’Load `{scoringRulesFile}` to determine category scores:
   149в†’
   150в†’- **Export Coverage:** (documented / total_exports) * 100
   151в†’- **Signature Accuracy:** (matching_signatures / total_documented) * 100 (Forge/Deep only, "N/A" for Quick)
   152в†’- **Type Coverage:** (documented_types / total_types) * 100 (Forge/Deep only, "N/A" for Quick)
   153в†’
   154в†’### 5. Append Coverage Analysis to Output
   155в†’
   156в†’Append the **Coverage Analysis** section to `{outputFile}`:
   157в†’
   158в†’```markdown
   159в†’## Coverage Analysis
   160в†’
   161в†’**Tier:** {forge_tier}
   162в†’**Source Access:** {analysis_confidence} (full | provenance-map | metadata-only | remote-only | docs-only)
   163в†’**Source Path:** {source_path}
   164в†’**Files Analyzed:** {count}
   165в†’
   166в†’### Export Coverage
   167в†’
   168в†’| Export | Type | Documented | Signature | Source Location | Status |
   169в†’|--------|------|-----------|-----------|-----------------|--------|
   170в†’| ... per-export rows ... |
   171в†’
   172в†’### Coverage Summary
   173в†’
   174в†’- **Exports Found:** {N}
   175в†’- **Documented:** {N} ({percentage}%)
   176в†’- **Missing Documentation:** {N}
   177в†’- **Signature Mismatches:** {N}
   178в†’- **Stale Documentation:** {N}
   179в†’
   180в†’### Category Scores
   181в†’
   182в†’| Category | Score | Weight | Weighted |
   183в†’|----------|-------|--------|----------|
   184в†’| Export Coverage | {N}% | {weight}% | {weighted}% |
   185в†’| Signature Accuracy | {N}% | {weight}% | {weighted}% |
   186в†’| Type Coverage | {N}% | {weight}% | {weighted}% |
   187в†’```
   188в†’
   189в†’### 6. Report Coverage Results
   190в†’
   191в†’"**Coverage check complete.**
   192в†’
   193в†’**{skill_name}** вЂ” {forge_tier} tier analysis of {file_count} source files:
   194в†’
   195в†’- Exports: {documented}/{total} documented ({percentage}%)
   196в†’- Signatures: {matching}/{total} accurate ({percentage}% or N/A for Quick)
   197в†’- Types: {documented_types}/{total_types} covered ({percentage}% or N/A for Quick)
   198в†’
   199в†’**{N} issues found** вЂ” details in Coverage Analysis section.
   200в†’
   201в†’**Proceeding to coherence check...**"
   202в†’
   203в†’### 7. Auto-Proceed
   204в†’
   205в†’Display: "**Proceeding to coherence check...**"
   206в†’
   207в†’#### Menu Handling Logic:
   208в†’
   209в†’- After coverage analysis is complete, update {outputFile} frontmatter stepsCompleted, then immediately load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
   210в†’
   211в†’#### EXECUTION RULES:
   212в†’
   213в†’- This is an auto-proceed validation step with no user choices
   214в†’- Proceed directly to next step after coverage is analyzed
   215в†’
   216в†’## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   217в†’
   218в†’ONLY WHEN all source files have been analyzed, the Coverage Analysis section has been appended to {outputFile}, and category scores have been calculated, will you then load and read fully `{nextStepFile}` to execute coherence check.
   219в†’
   220в†’---
   221в†’
   222в†’## рџљЁ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   223в†’
   224в†’### вњ… SUCCESS:
   225в†’
   226в†’- All source files analyzed at appropriate tier depth
   227в†’- Every finding has file:line citation (Forge/Deep) or file-level reference (Quick)
   228в†’- Per-export status table complete

   233в†’### вќЊ SYSTEM FAILURE:

   242в†’**Master Rule:** Skipping steps, optimizing sequences, or not following exact instructions is FORBIDDEN and constitutes SYSTEM FAILURE. Zero hallucination вЂ” every finding traces to code.
